Class Shelter

java.lang.Object
shelter.domain.Shelter
All Implemented Interfaces:
Comparable<Shelter>
public class Shelter extends Object implements Comparable<Shelter>
Represents an animal shelter that holds a collection of animals available for adoption. A shelter enforces a maximum capacity and provides operations to add, remove, query, and list the animals it currently holds.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors

    Description
    Shelter(String name, String location, int capacity)
    Constructs a new Shelter with the given name, location, and capacity.

    Reconstruction constructor for deserializing a Shelter from persistent storage.
    Shelter(Shelter other)
    Copy constructor that creates a new Shelter with all field values copied from other.

  • Constructor Details

    • Shelter

      public Shelter(String id, String name, String location, int capacity)
      Reconstruction constructor for deserializing a Shelter from persistent storage. This constructor preserves the original id so that cross-references between animals and shelters remain consistent after a round-trip through CSV.
      Parameters:
      id - the pre-existing unique identifier; must not be null or blank
      name - the shelter's name; must not be null or blank
      location - the shelter's physical address or city; must not be null or blank
      capacity - the maximum number of animals the shelter can hold; must be positive
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any parameter is null, blank, or capacity is not positive

    • Shelter

      public Shelter(String name, String location, int capacity)
      Constructs a new Shelter with the given name, location, and capacity. Name and location must be non-null and non-blank; capacity must be a positive integer.
      Parameters:
      name - the shelter's name; must not be null or blank
      location - the shelter's physical address or city; must not be null or blank
      capacity - the maximum number of animals the shelter can hold; must be positive
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any parameter is null, blank, or capacity is not positive

    • Shelter

      public Shelter(Shelter other)
      Copy constructor that creates a new Shelter with all field values copied from other. The animal list is shallow-copied so the new shelter holds references to the same Animal objects.
      Parameters:
      other - the Shelter instance to copy; must not be null

  • Method Details

    • addAnimal

      public void addAnimal(Animal animal)
      Adds an animal to this shelter if capacity allows. The same animal (identified by ID) cannot be added twice to the same shelter.
      Parameters:
      animal - the animal to add; must not be null
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if animal is null or is already present in this shelter
      IllegalStateException - if the shelter is at full capacity

    • removeAnimal

      public void removeAnimal(String animalId)
      Removes the animal with the given ID from this shelter. Throws if no animal with the given ID is currently in the shelter.
      Parameters:
      animalId - the ID of the animal to remove; must not be null
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if animalId is null or no matching animal is found

    • containsAnimal

      public boolean containsAnimal(String animalId)
      Returns whether an animal with the given ID is currently in this shelter.
      Parameters:
      animalId - the ID to search for; must not be null
      Returns:
      true if the animal is present, false otherwise
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if animalId is null

    • hasCapacity

      public boolean hasCapacity()
      Returns whether this shelter has remaining capacity for at least one more animal.
      Returns:
      true if the shelter can accept more animals, false if at capacity

    • getAnimals

      public List<Animal> getAnimals()
      Returns an unmodifiable view of all animals currently in this shelter. The returned list reflects the state of the shelter at the time of the call; callers must not attempt to modify the returned list.
      Returns:
      an unmodifiable List of Animal objects

    • getId

      public String getId()
      Returns the unique identifier of this shelter.
      Returns:
      the UUID string identifying this shelter

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Returns the name of this shelter.
      Returns:
      the shelter's name

    • getLocation

      public String getLocation()
      Returns the physical location of this shelter.
      Returns:
      the shelter's location

    • getCapacity

      public int getCapacity()
      Returns the maximum number of animals this shelter can hold.
      Returns:
      the shelter's capacity

    • getCurrentCount

      public int getCurrentCount()
      Returns the current number of animals in this shelter.
      Returns:
      the current animal count, between 0 and getCapacity() inclusive

    • compareTo

      public int compareTo(Shelter other)
      Compares this shelter to another by name alphabetically.
      Specified by:
      compareTo in interface Comparable<Shelter>
      Parameters:
      other - the other Shelter to compare to
      Returns:
      a negative number if this name comes first, positive if later, zero if equal

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object o)
      Returns true if the given object is a Shelter with the same ID. Shelter identity is determined solely by UUID.
      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
      Parameters:
      o - the object to compare
      Returns:
      true if o is a Shelter with the same ID

    • hashCode

      public int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code based on this shelter's unique ID. Consistent with equals(Object).
      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object
      Returns:
      the hash code

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this shelter including its ID, name, location, and capacity.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      a human-readable description of this shelter
